title: Professional Email
template:
Write a professional email to [recipient/role] about [topic]. The tone should be [formal/friendly/urgent]. Key points to include:
- [point 1]
- [point 2]
- [point 3]

Keep it concise, and end with a clear call to action.

title: Follow-Up Email
template: Write a polite follow-up email to [recipient] regarding [previous topic/meeting]. It has been [timeframe] since we last spoke. Remind them of [key context] and ask about [next step/question].

title: Meeting Summary
template:
Summarize the following meeting notes into a clear, structured summary with sections for Key Decisions, Action Items (with owners and deadlines), and Open Questions:

[paste raw meeting notes here]

title: Meeting Agenda
template: Create a meeting agenda for a [duration]-minute meeting about [topic]. Attendees: [list attendees]. Goals for the meeting: [goal 1], [goal 2]. Include time allocations for each agenda item.

title: Excel Formula Help
template: I need an Excel formula that does the following: [describe what you want, e.g. 'sums values in column B where column A equals a specific text value']. My data is structured as: [describe columns/ranges]. Please explain the formula and how it works.

title: Data Cleaning Steps
template: I have a spreadsheet with the following issues: [describe issues, e.g. duplicate rows, inconsistent date formats, extra whitespace]. Walk me through step-by-step how to clean this data in Excel, including any formulas or tools I should use.

title: SWOT Analysis
template: Create a SWOT analysis for [company/product/project]. Consider the following context: [brief background]. Structure the output into four clear sections: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats, with 3-5 bullet points each.

title: Market Research Summary
template: Research and summarize the current market landscape for [industry/product category]. Include: market size and growth trends, key player, target customer segments, and emerging opportunities or risks. Cite general knowledge; note where data may be outdated.

title: Competitor Analysis
template: Compare [our company/product] against these competitors: [competitor 1], [competitor 2], [competitor 3]. For each, analyze: pricing, key features, target audience, and perceived strengths/weaknesses. End with a summary of our competitive positioning.

title: Acquisition Summary
template: Summarize the potential strategic rationale for [Company A] acquiring [Company B]. Consider: market synergies, product/technology fit, financial implications, and integration risks. Present this as a brief executive summary.

title: Presentation Outline
template: Create a slide-by-slide outline for a [duration]-minute presentation on [topic] for an audience of [audience type]. Include a title for each slide and 2-3 bullet points of key content per slide. End with a strong closing/call-to-action slide.

title: Executive Summary Slide
template: Write the content for a single executive summary slide covering [topic/project]. It should answer: what is it, why does it matter, what's the ask/next step. Keep it to 4-5 concise bullet points suitable for a slide.

title: Rewrite for Clarity
template:
Rewrite the following text to be clearer, more concise, and more professional, without changing its meaning:

[paste text here]

title: Summarize a Document
template:
Summarize the following document into [3-5] key bullet points suitable for someone who doesn't have time to read the full thing:

[paste document text here]

title: Pros and Cons List
template: Create a balanced pros and cons list for [decision/option], considering factors like [cost, time, risk, team impact, etc.]. End with a brief recommendation based on the most likely priorities of [stakeholder/team].

title: Root Cause Analysis
template: Help me think through the root cause of the following problem: [describe the problem]. Use a 'five whys' style approach to dig past the surface symptom to the underlying cause, and suggest possible solutions.

title: Performance Review Draft
template: Help me draft a performance review for [employee name/role]. Their key accomplishments this period were: [accomplishment 1], [accomplishment 2]. Areas for growth include: [area 1]. Keep the tone constructive and specific, avoiding vague generalities.

title: Job Description
template: Write a job description for a [job title] role on our [team name] team. Key responsibilities include: [responsibility 1], [responsibility 2]. Required qualifications: [qualification 1], [qualification 2]. Keep the tone professional but approachable.

title: Customer Support Response
template: Write a helpful, empathetic response to a customer who is experiencing [issue description]. Acknowledge their frustration, explain [solution/next step], and offer [compensation/follow-up] if appropriate.

title: Customer FAQ Entry
template: Write a clear, concise FAQ entry answering the question: '[customer question]'. The answer should be friendly, accurate, and no more than 3-4 sentences, written for a general customer audience.

title: Cold Outreach Email
template: Write a short, personalized cold outreach email to [prospect name/role] at [company name]. Reference [relevant context, e.g. recent news or shared connection]. Clearly state the value proposition of [product/service] in 1-2 sentences, and end with a low-pressure call to action like scheduling a brief call.

title: Sales Objection Response
template: A prospect raised this objection: '[objection, e.g. too expensive / not the right time / already using a competitor]'. Help me write a thoughtful response that acknowledges their concern and reframes the value of [product/service] without being pushy.

title: Proposal Summary
template: Write an executive summary for a sales proposal to [client/company name]. Include: the problem they're facing ([problem]), our proposed solution ([solution]), expected outcomes/ROI, and a suggested next step. Keep it to one tight paragraph suitable for the top of a proposal document.

title: Plain-English Contract Summary
template:
Summarize the following contract or legal clause into plain, non-legal English so a non-lawyer can understand the key obligations, rights, and risks:

[paste contract text here]

Note: this is for general understanding only and is not a substitute for professional legal review.

title: NDA Request Email
template: Write a professional email to [recipient] requesting that they sign an NDA before we proceed with discussions about [topic/project]. Keep the tone collaborative, not overly formal or intimidating.

title: Policy Explanation
template:
Explain the following company policy in simple, friendly language suitable for an internal FAQ or onboarding doc:

[paste policy text here]

Include a short example of how it applies in a common real-world situation.

title: Budget Variance Explanation
template: Explain the variance between budgeted and actual spend for [department/project] this [month/quarter]. Budgeted: [amount]. Actual: [amount]. Key drivers of the difference: [driver 1], [driver 2]. Write this as a short paragraph suitable for a finance review meeting.

title: Expense Report Summary
template:
Summarize the following list of expenses into categories with subtotals, and flag anything that looks unusually high or potentially non-compliant with standard travel/expense policy:

[paste expense list here]

title: Financial Metric Explainer
template: Explain what [financial metric, e.g. EBITDA, gross margin, churn rate, CAC] means in simple terms, why it matters for [type of business], and give one example of how a change in this metric would affect the business.

title: New Hire Welcome Email
template: Write a warm welcome email to [new hire name], who is joining as [role] on [team name], starting [start date]. Include: what to expect on day one, who their main point of contact is ([manager/buddy name]), and one or two things that make the team/company culture special.

title: 30-60-90 Day Plan
template: Create a 30-60-90 day onboarding plan for a new [job title] joining the [team name] team. Include specific goals and learning milestones for each phase (30 days: [focus area], 60 days: [focus area], 90 days: [focus area]).

title: Team Overview Doc
template: Write a short 'team overview' document for new hires joining the [team name] team. Include: the team's mission, key responsibilities, main tools/systems used ([tool 1], [tool 2]), and who's who on the team.

title: Project Status Update
template: Write a concise project status update for [project name] to send to [stakeholders]. Structure it as: Overall Status ([on track/at risk/delayed]), Key Accomplishments This Period, Upcoming Milestones, and Risks/Blockers. Keep it skimmable, not a wall of text.

title: Risk Assessment
template: Identify potential risks for the project '[project name]', which involves [brief project description]. For each risk, note the likelihood (low/medium/high), potential impact, and a suggested mitigation strategy.

title: Retrospective Summary
template:
Summarize the following retrospective notes from [project/sprint name] into three clear sections: What Went Well, What Didn't Go Well, and Action Items for Next Time (with owners if mentioned):

[paste raw retro notes here]

steps.label: Step 1: Market Landscape
steps.template: Research and summarize the current market landscape for [industry/product category]. Include market size, growth trends, and key players.

steps.label: Step 2: Customer Segments
steps.template: Based on the market landscape above, identify the 3-4 most important target customer segments for [product/company]. For each, describe their needs, pain points, and buying behavior.

steps.label: Step 3: Opportunity Summary
steps.template: Using the market landscape and customer segments above, summarize the top 3 opportunities [company] should pursue, and the top 2 risks to watch out for.

steps.label: Step 1: Account Research
steps.template: Research [company name] and summarize: what they do, recent news, likely priorities this year, and any signals they might need [our product/service].

steps.label: Step 2: Talking Points
steps.template: Based on the research above, write 3-4 talking points for a discovery call with [prospect name/role] at [company name], focused on their likely priorities.

steps.label: Step 3: Follow-Up Email
steps.template: Write a follow-up email to [prospect name] after our discovery call, referencing [key point discussed] and proposing [next step].

title: Employment Verification Response
template: Draft a professional response to an employment verification request for [employee name]. Confirm only: job title ([title]), employment dates ([start date] to [end date, or 'present']), and employment status ([status]). Do not include any information beyond what was specifically requested.

title: Termination Documentation Checklist
template: Create a checklist for processing the termination of [employee name/role], separating for reason: [reason for separation]. Include standard steps like updating employment records, notifying relevant systems ([systems, e.g. payroll, benefits]), and required documentation to complete. Flag anything that should be double-checked with HR policy before finalizing.

title: Unemployment Hearing Prep Summary
template:
Summarize the key facts for an unemployment hearing regarding [employee name]'s separation from the company. Include: reason for separation ([reason]), relevant dates, and a clear, factual timeline based on the following notes:

[paste employment history / separation notes here]

Keep the tone factual and neutral, suitable for presenting to a hearing referee.

title: Payroll Deduction Reconciliation Explainer
template:
Help me reconcile the following payroll deductions against expected amounts for [pay period]. Deduction types: [medical/dental/vision/other]. Here is the data:

[paste deduction data here]

Identify any mismatches or amounts that look off, and explain what might be causing each discrepancy.

title: Investigation Findings Summary
template:
Summarize the findings of an internal investigation into [type of complaint, e.g. fraud, conflict of interest, policy violation]. Structure the summary into: Background, Findings, and Recommended Next Steps, based on the following notes:

[paste investigation notes here]

Keep the tone neutral and factual. Note: this is a draft for internal review only and should be verified against full documentation before being finalized.

title: Regulatory Research Digest
template: Research and summarize recent changes to [specific regulation/topic, e.g. state labor law, HIPAA guidance] relevant to [industry/department]. For each key point, note the source so it can be independently verified before being relied on.

title: Deal Workup Structure
template: Create a structure/outline for a preliminary financial workup on a potential acquisition target: [company/target name]. Include sections for: company overview, key financials to gather ([revenue, EBITDA, etc.]), market position, and initial risk considerations. This is a starting framework — actual figures should come from verified source documents, not be estimated by AI.

title: Market Expansion Research Brief
template: Research the market opportunity for expanding [company/service line] into [new market/region]. Include: market size/demand signals, potential competitors already operating there, and regulatory or licensing considerations to be aware of. Flag anything that should be verified with local/regional sources before acting on it.

title: Social Caption & Flyer Copy
template: Write [3] short social media caption options and one flyer headline for [campaign/topic]. Audience: [target audience]. Tone: [brand tone, e.g. warm, professional, energetic]. Keep captions under 200 characters each.

title: Branded Image Generation Brief
template: Write a detailed image generation prompt for creating a [type of visual, e.g. social poster, flyer, brochure image] featuring [subject, e.g. a caregiver with a client]. Style: [brand style, e.g. warm and photorealistic]. Include: setting, mood, color palette, and any text/logo placement notes.

title: Inbox Triage & To-Do List
template:
Organize the following forwarded emails into a prioritized to-do list, grouped by urgency (Urgent / This Week / Can Wait). For each item, note who it's from and what action is needed:

[paste email subject lines/summaries here]

title: State Regulation Summary for Operations
template:
Summarize the following state regulation or budget document into the key points relevant to [department/operations area]. Highlight anything that requires an operational change or compliance action, and note which parts should be verified with legal/compliance before acting:

[paste regulation/budget text here]
